title: UAP Disclosure Tracker Update — Apr 18, 2026
summary: The Pentagon missed Rep. Luna's April 14 deadline for 46 classified UAP videos, prompting a bipartisan backlash and subpoena threats from the House Oversight Task Force. The War Department confirmed that AARO is coordinating with the White House on 'never-before-seen' UAP material, though no videos or timeline were provided. Rep. Burchett disclosed that classified briefers named 'names, dates, people and locations' of alleged UAP programs, and Rep. Moskowitz demanded two additional congressional hearings. With 32 days until Trump's executive order 90-day milestone (May 20, 2026), the gap between White House rhetoric and Pentagon action remains the defining tension. Breaking status set due to active congressional standoff.

title: UAP Disclosure Tracker Update — Tue Apr 7, 2026
UAP Disclosure Tracker — Initial Publication
summary: Launched UAP Disclosure Tracker covering 79 years of UAP/UFO history from the 1947 Roswell incident through the 2026 Trump disclosure executive order. Tracker documents congressional hearings, whistleblower testimony, AARO investigations, and the ongoing institutional process of UAP disclosure. 6 KPIs, 5 contested claims, 10 key political figures, and a comprehensive timeline spanning 5 eras.
